title: Stability-weighted expert blend selected under both grouped and forward CV
summary: Scikit-learn’s GroupKFold and TimeSeriesSplit highlight different leakage risks: same-group contamination versus future-to-past leakage.
rationale: Scikit-learn’s GroupKFold and TimeSeriesSplit highlight different leakage risks: same-group contamination versus future-to-past leakage. Given the small gains left, leaderboard-safe improvement is likely to come from keeping only models that are strong under both race-group and temporal splits, then blending rank-diverse experts rather than chasing a single CV setup.
implementation_hint: Evaluate each expert under `GroupKFold` by race-like groups and a forward split by year/order. Keep only models whose fold metrics are stable across both. Blend by rank averaging or OOF-stacked logits, then optionally apply fold-wise calibration only to reduce ties or stabilize probabilities, not to chase AUC directly.
expected_effect: Lower CV illusion risk and a better chance that public-LB gains survive distribution shift between races/years.
risk: This may improve robustness more than local CV, so it can look neutral locally while still being the safer competition move.
